summary: Frontend-only testing of AUKTAVE 2K26 Next.js site at desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (375x812). All 11 routes return correct HTTP status (200 / 404). Splash screen, hero, navbar, audio controller, announcement banner dismiss + persistence, all 7 event cards, Hackathon/Robotics/IEEE event-detail pages with rules + coordinator + register CTA + rules-download, sponsorship tiers + brochure + proposal PDFs (HEAD 200), register form with all 7 required fields + web3forms warning + visible register-error after submit (CORS/placeholder key as expected), help form + Leaflet map + FAQ accordion, privacy & terms pages, 404 not-found-page with Hawkins copy, and StickyMobileCTA visible on /, /about, /events but NOT /register at 375px - all WORKING. Found 1 CRITICAL mobile UX bug and 1 minor asset bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Mobile menu toggle (data-testid=mobile-menu-toggle) is NOT clickable when AnnouncementBanner is visible (the default state for first-time visitors). The banner has 'relative z-50' while the Navbar has 'fixed top-0 z-40', so the banner's inner div intercepts pointer events at the toggle's coordinates (y~12px). Real user click times out: '<div class="max-w-7xl ... h-9 ..."> from <div data-testid="announcement-banner" ...> subtree intercepts pointer events'. Confirmed reproducible after localStorage.clear(). Once banner is dismissed, toggle works fine. Also visually the banner covers the navbar logo on desktop too.

FALLBACK_AUDIO URL https://cdn.pixabay.com/audio/2022/03/15/audio_1718e1d3a8.mp3 returns HTTP 403. Since NEXT_PUBLIC_AUDIO_URL is empty, every page logs a 403 console error and audio playback never works in default config.

design_issues.screen: All pages (mobile + desktop) when banner not yet dismissed
issues: Announcement banner stacks over navbar (z-50 vs z-40) overlapping the logo and menu button instead of pushing navbar down. Either move banner above navbar in fixed flow with navbar offset (top-9), reduce banner z-index below navbar, or render banner inside the fixed navbar.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Re-order/restyle AnnouncementBanner vs fixed Navbar so the banner does NOT cover the mobile menu toggle. Suggested: give Navbar z-50 (>= banner) OR position banner above the fixed navbar by offsetting Navbar with top-9 when banner is visible OR put the banner inside the Navbar container.
FIX (MEDIUM): Replace broken Pixabay fallback audio URL or host a small public mp3 in /public. Current 403 happens on every page for every visitor.

Optional polish: 'Hawkins' eyebrow on 404 starts visible but the GlitchText h2 'Something went wrong in Hawkins' uses framer-motion whileInView with initial opacity 0 - text is in DOM/HTML so not a real bug, just slow first-paint.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar z-40 + AnnouncementBanner z-50 in same fixed/relative layer is the root cause of the mobile menu blocking bug.
AudioController.jsx hardcodes a third-party CDN audio URL as fallback - that asset is not stable (403). Self-host instead.

AnnouncementBanner uses 'relative z-50' but lives above a fixed-positioned Navbar in document flow; visually they collide because Navbar is fixed top-0 - banner ends up rendered AT the same screen position as navbar instead of pushing it down.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 functional checks from review_request pass except the mobile menu interaction when the announcement banner is visible. To reproduce: set viewport 375x812 -> goto /about -> localStorage.clear() -> reload -> click [data-testid=mobile-menu-toggle] -> click intercepted by announcement-banner div. Web3Forms submission failure with placeholder key correctly surfaces register-error - this is expected behavior, not a bug. Brochure PDFs at /brochures/sponsorship-brochure.pdf and /brochures/sponsorship-proposal.pdf return 200. The 404 page does contain 'Hawkins' and 'Something went wrong' (verified in HTML).
rca of the issue: AnnouncementBanner is rendered before <Navbar/> in /app/frontend/app/layout.jsx (lines 35-36). Banner uses className 'relative z-50'. Navbar uses 'fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-40'. Because Navbar is fixed, it's removed from normal flow and pinned to viewport top - the same position the banner occupies. With z-50 > z-40, the banner's inner DIV captures pointer events on top of the navbar's hamburger button (at x=315,y=12 on a 375px viewport). Mitigation options: (1) raise Navbar z-index to z-50 and ensure it includes/sits above banner, (2) render the banner inside a wrapping fixed container that includes the navbar so navbar gets pushed down with top: banner-height, (3) make Navbar non-fixed when banner is visible, (4) reduce banner z-index below navbar (z-30) and accept that scrolled navbar will cover banner.
